intro: Account currency is the currency assigned to an account for recording, maintaining, reconciling, and controlling financial balances inside an enterprise finance or ERP system.
doctrine_statement:
Account currency is not merely a configuration field.
It is the control point where external monetary movement enters accounting custody.
primary_section_title: Account currency governs how balances are held, controlled, revalued, and defended.
primary_section_body: In enterprise finance, an account does not only hold a balance. It holds a balance under a currency identity. That identity governs how postings are recorded, how balances are maintained, how foreign currency amounts are revalued, and how the account can be reconciled and audited.

Account currency is therefore different from transaction currency, settlement currency, functional currency, ledger currency, reporting currency, and consolidation currency. These are related but distinct roles inside the currency state chain.
AccountCcy.com treats account currency as a control discipline because errors or weak governance at this layer can affect the ledger, reporting, consolidation, and audit reality downstream.
graph_title: Account currency is State 04 of the CCY State Chain.
graph_intro: It is the central control point between external financial movement and internal accounting record. This is where AccountCcy.com lives conceptually and strategically.
grid_title: Account currency is State 04 of the CCY State Chain.
grid_intro: It is the central control point between external financial movement and internal accounting record. This is where AccountCcy.com lives conceptually and strategically.

cards.label: Not Transaction Currency
cards.title: Transaction currency is the currency of the event.
cards.body: It defines the currency in which the financial obligation is denominated. It does not necessarily define how an account balance is maintained.

cards.label: Not Settlement Currency
cards.title: Settlement currency is the currency of cash transfer.
cards.body: It defines how payment occurs. It may differ from transaction currency and account currency, creating FX consequences.

cards.label: Not Ledger Currency
cards.title: Ledger currency is the currency of maintained books.
cards.body: It often reflects functional currency policy and may require foreign currency account balances to be revalued.

cards.label: Not Reporting Currency
cards.title: Reporting currency is the currency of presentation.
cards.body: It governs how results are shown to management, regulators, investors, or group reporting structures.

cards.label: Control Point
cards.title: Account currency is where movement becomes record.
cards.body: It is the custody gate through which external monetary activity enters internal accounting control.

cards.label: Audit Consequence
cards.title: Account currency affects whether the final number can be defended.
cards.body: Weak account currency governance can create downstream traceability, reconciliation, and audit problems.

control_title: Account currency turns currency identity into accounting custody.
control_body: A strong account currency layer clarifies what currency an account is allowed to maintain, how balances should be interpreted, and how foreign currency movements should be controlled through the close process.
risk_title: Misunderstanding account currency creates structural risk.
risk_body: If account currency is confused with transaction currency, settlement currency, ledger currency, or reporting currency, the result can be revaluation errors, reconciliation breaks, reporting distortion, and audit-trail gaps.

faq.question: Is account currency the same as transaction currency?
faq.answer: No. Transaction currency is the currency in which the financial event is denominated. Account currency is the currency assigned to the account where balances are recorded or maintained.

faq.question: Is account currency the same as functional currency?
faq.answer: Not necessarily. Functional currency relates to the primary economic environment of the entity. Account currency is an account-level currency assignment inside the accounting or ERP system.

faq.question: Why does account currency matter?
faq.answer: It matters because it influences balance maintenance, revaluation, reconciliation, reporting, and audit defensibility.

schema_notes.framework_status: Reference definition within the AccountCcy framework.
